content: If we map the lightweight, training-free iterative self-feedback mechanism of Self-Refine to the dynamic memory architecture of Reflexion agents, we can build an autonomous agent that continuously improves its long-term task performance without external human supervision or additional reward model training. This creates a recursive self-improvement loop where short-run output refinement from Self-Refine feeds into Reflexion’s long-term memory to refine future decision-making.
